mission.title: Install and Configure Kairos on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kairos is an immutable Linux meta-distribution designed for edge Kubernetes, enabling the creation of bootable Kubernetes and OS images for edge devices. Installing Kairos allows you to manage cluster lifecycle seamlessly and securely, ensuring no infrastructure drift.

steps.title: Create the Kairos Namespace
steps.description: First, create a dedicated namespace for Kairos to isolate its resources.

steps.title: Apply Kairos Manifests
steps.description: Next, apply the Kairos manifests from the GitHub repository to set up the necessary resources.

steps.title: Verify Installation
steps.description: After applying the manifests, verify that the Kairos pods are running correctly.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Kairos will show the Kairos pods running in the 'kairos' namespace. You should also be able to access the service via port 8080 on your local machine.

uninstall.title: Remove Kairos Resources
uninstall.description: To uninstall Kairos, delete the resources applied in the Kairos namespace.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kairos
upgrade.description: To upgrade Kairos, apply the new version of the manifests. Ensure to specify the new version in the URLs.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description: If the Kairos pods are not starting, check the pod logs for errors using the command: `kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kairos`.

troubleshooting.title: Resource Quotas
troubleshooting.description: Ensure that your cluster has enough resources (CPU, Memory) available. Check resource quotas with: `kubectl describe quota -n kairos`.

troubleshooting.title: Network Issues
troubleshooting.description: If the pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, investigate potential network issues or misconfigurations in your cluster networking.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have kubectl installed and configured to communicate with your Kubernetes cluster.
